questions.question: What is India's current share in global antiretroviral drug supply for HIV/AIDS?
questions.explanation: India supplies between 70-80% of the global antiretroviral (ARV) drugs used for HIV/AIDS treatment. India is the leading producer of generic ARV medicines worldwide. As of 2023, Indian pharmaceutical firms manufacture WHO-GMP certified ARVs exported to over 100 countries. Over 30.7 million people globally receive treatment with ARVs produced in India.

questions.question: What is India's share in the global vaccines market?
questions.explanation: India produces about 60% of the world's vaccine supply. India accounts for around 60% of vaccines supplied to UNICEF globally. Serum Institute of India is the world's largest vaccine manufacturer by doses produced. In 2025, India's global vaccines market share by value was approximately 3.7%, but it leads in production volume.

questions.question: What is India's projected position in global API exports by 2047?

options.label: A
options.text: India will lead with a 40% market share

options.label: B
options.text: India ranks fifth in API production capacity

options.label: C
options.text: India may reach $80-90 billion in API exports

options.label: D
options.text: India has no significant export role in APIs

answer.label: C
answer.text: India may reach $80-90 billion in API exports

questions.explanation: China leads the outsourced API market with about a 35% share. India is projected to increase its API exports from approximately $12 billion in 2030 to $80-90 billion by 2047. The Indian domestic API market is valued at $14.18 billion in 2025 and is expected to reach $22.18 billion by 2031. India is among the top global players in API production and export potential.

questions.question: What is the FDI allowed in brownfield pharma under the automatic route?
questions.explanation: Indian government allows 74% in the brownfield pharms under the automatic route and thereafter through the government approval route. 100% Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) under the automatic route for greenfield pharma.

questions.question: What is India's share in global generic medicines supply?
questions.explanation: India supplies about 20% of global generic medicines as of 2023, making it the largest supplier worldwide. The Indian pharmaceutical industry consists of over 10,000 manufacturing facilities. India provides around 40% of generic drugs used in the United States and about 33% in the United Kingdom. The sector includes over 3,000 pharmaceutical companies. The country is often called the “pharmacy of the world.”
